title: Trump Faces Blowback on Easing Iran Oil Sanctions
summary: President Trump once assailed the Obama administration for making cash payments to Iran. Now he supports sanctions relief that could give the country a $14 billion windfall.

title: Trump Delays Threat to Iran, but War Negotiations Are in Early Stage
summary: President Trump postponed his threat to strike power plants in Iran, citing “productive conversations” with the Iranians. But officials said the talks were in an early stage and not substantive.

title: Trump Sours on Keir Starmer Over UK’s Decision Not to Join Attacks on Iran
summary: President Trump once called Prime Minister Keir Starmer a friend. But Britain’s decision not to join the attacks on Iran has led to merciless mocking by the president.

title: A New Phase of the War in Iran, and the Latest on the LaGuardia Plane Crash
summary: Plus, how dancing the tango can help patients with Parkinson’s disease.

title: In LaGuardia Crash That Killed 2, Call to ‘Stop!’ Came Too Late
summary: A collision between an Air Canada Express jet and a fire truck on Sunday night left two pilots dead and dozens injured.

title: College Graduates Are Facing the Grimmest Job Market in Years
summary: Artificial intelligence could reshape work, but for now a low-hire, low-fire labor market is the main impediment for young people seeking employment.

title: Gregory Bovino’s Final Days: Harsh Words and Few Regrets
summary: He was the face of the Trump administration’s immigration crackdown. But as he begins a retirement that was not entirely voluntary, the Border Patrol leader says he did not go far enough.

title: When Trump Wants Something Done, He Dispatches ICE to Do It
summary: President Trump has increasingly used Immigration and Customs Enforcement to push personal and political objectives, and on Monday sent agents to airports across the country to help deal with long security lines.

title: 34 Former Military Members Were Put on Deportation Track in the Past Year
summary: The Trump administration has ramped up enforcement against immigrant service members and their families in its wider crackdown.

title: Tango Therapy: How the Dance of Passion Is Helping Parkinson’s Patients
summary: Once a week, patients in an Argentine hospital with Parkinson’s disease use the movements of tango to help address issues of balance, stiffness and coordination.

title: Afghanistan Frees American Detainee Amid Mounting U.S. Pressure
summary: Dennis Walter Coyle, a researcher held since last year, was released weeks after the United States declared Afghanistan a “state sponsor of wrongful detention.”

title: Should You Need to Prove Citizenship to Vote? Ask Kansas.
summary: A Kansas law required a passport, a birth certificate or other proof of citizenship to register, but it was struck down after a court found that around 31,000 eligible voters had been blocked.

title: A Veteran Group Jumps In for a Democrat in Iowa Senate Race.
summary: VoteVets is the first super PAC to intervene in the race for Josh Turek, a state legislator who was born with spina bifida after his father was exposed to Agent Orange in Vietnam.

title: Trump, Who Calls Mail-in Voting ‘Cheating,’ Just Voted by Mail
summary: President Trump has long fixated on mail-in voting to bolster his baseless claims of widespread voter fraud. But he recently used the method in a Florida special election.

title: Chavez Revelations Force Teachers to Rethink How They Teach His Legacy
summary: In classrooms across the country, educators are weighing whether to shift focus from Cesar Chavez to the broader labor movement he helped lead.

title: Reality TV Confronts a Harsh TV Reality
summary: The number of unscripted series has plummeted by a third since 2022. As the industry rapidly changes, an era is quietly vanishing.

title: Government Cuts Gut the Memory of Argentina’s Dirty War
summary: Fifty years after the military dictatorship, Argentina’s government is defunding human rights groups and promoting a revisionist account of the junta’s crimes.

title: Wicked Stepmother No Longer, a Female Pharoah Gets a Reputational Makeover
summary: A reassessment of damaged 3,500-year-old statuary adds to evidence that Queen Hatshepsut wasn’t the villain that scholars long took her to be.

title: MDMA Therapy in Australia Shows Results for PTSD Patients, but the Cost Is Limiting Access
summary: The country’s experiment with psychedelic medicine has led to positive outcomes, psychiatrists say, but also highlights the limitations of the nascent field.

title: The United States Is No Longer the Leader of the Free World
summary: Pax Americana, meet Lax Americana.

title: The Deadly Gender Gap in Car Safety
summary: For over half a century, car safety standards have left women’s lives in the rearview.

title: Health Woes
summary: We look into the turmoil at the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention.

title: Late Night Doesn’t Feel So Hot About ICE at the Airport
summary: Jimmy Kimmel said President Trump had “found a way to make the airport even worse.”

title: Plane Crash Kills 66 From Military and Police in Colombia
summary: A military aircraft transporting 128 troops and crew members was in an accident as it took off from southern Colombia, military officials said.

title: Boston University Pulls Pride Flags, Raising Free Speech Worries
summary: The university said the flags broke a rule against hanging signs, a policy embraced by other campuses that cracked down on protests. Professors and others say such rules chill speech.

title: As Denmark Goes to the Polls, Here’s What to Know About the Election and Key Issues
summary: President Trump’s threats to take Greenland away from Denmark have lifted Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen, who appeared the front-runner as polls opened.

title: Pakistan Dials Up Its Information War
summary: New, friendly media operations and expanded state-run television are pushing Pakistan’s message while independent news outlets face repression.
